method: Same 4-fold walk-forward (fixed thr 0.75 + regime gate) as the combined model; dataset split by direction.
verdict: REJECT - direction-specific models are WORSE than the combined model
analysis: Combined net +₹151,630 / 359 tr (+₹422/tr) vs direction-specific sum +₹96,566 / 2,459 tr (+₹39/tr).
Splitting the 1.26M-row dataset in half removes the direction feature as a discriminator (now constant per model) and halves training data → worse generalization.
SHORT-only model fires 2,017 trades at thr 0.75 (vs 312 SHORT in combined) — far less selective, +₹6/tr is noise.
Combined model is both MORE net-positive AND higher-quality per trade (extreme selectivity beats costs).
Phase C would DECREASE ML Standalone net PnL by ~36% (₹151,630 -> ₹96,566).
